title: MultiCharts vs TrendSpider (2026) — Which Is Better?
summary: Compare MultiCharts and TrendSpider — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

MultiCharts is a professional charting and automated trading platform built for experienced traders who want a one-time licensing fee model and portfolio-level backtesting. TrendSpider is a modern, AI-powered charting platform with subscription pricing that emphasizes automated analysis and trendline detection. The choice between them hinges on your workflow philosophy: do you want to own perpetual software with upfront costs, or subscribe to a cloud-based AI assistant?

## Pricing Comparison

MultiCharts charges a $1,497 one-time license fee with no recurring costs—a significant advantage for cost-conscious traders planning to use the platform for years. TrendSpider operates on a subscription model starting at $19 for a trial month, then $54/month for their base tier (cheaper on annual plans).

For annual cost comparison: MultiCharts is $1,497 upfront (then free forever), while TrendSpider costs $648/year minimum ($54/month × 12). If you use MultiCharts for three years, your effective annual cost is $499/year. Beyond year three, MultiCharts becomes dramatically cheaper. However, TrendSpider's subscription structure means you never pay a large upfront capital investment—important if you're bootstrapping or testing before committing. Neither platform offers a free tier; MultiCharts lists "Free" price but requires the $1,497 license purchase, while TrendSpider requires a paid trial. For traders on tight budgets, TrendSpider's lower entry barrier ($19 trial vs. $1,497) is significant.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Automated Charting Analysis**: TrendSpider's automated trendline and support/resistance detection is genuinely best-in-class and is the platform's differentiator—it saves hours of manual chart marking. MultiCharts requires manual setup unless you script it in EasyLanguage. Winner: TrendSpider.

**Backtesting & Historical Data**: TrendSpider offers 40+ years of historical data with 30K+ candles per test, supporting complex analysis across decades. MultiCharts excels at portfolio-level backtesting with genetic algorithm optimization, allowing you to test position sizing and correlation across multiple symbols simultaneously—critical for position traders. Winner: Depends on use case. MultiCharts for portfolio testing; TrendSpider for single-symbol, long-term backtesting.

**Broker Integration & Execution**: MultiCharts connects to dozens of brokers (Interactive Brokers, ThinkOrSwim, CQG, etc.) and handles trade execution directly. TrendSpider is analysis-only and requires you to connect a separate broker for execution, adding an extra step. Winner: MultiCharts.

**AI Assistance & Automation**: TrendSpider's Sidekick AI is a real market-aware assistant with live data access, not a generic chatbot. It understands your analysis and can suggest trade setups. MultiCharts offers no built-in AI assistant. Winner: TrendSpider.

**Visual Bot Building**: TrendSpider includes a visual bot builder for creating automated trading strategies without coding. MultiCharts requires EasyLanguage scripting to automate (steeper learning curve, but more powerful). Winner: TrendSpider for non-coders; MultiCharts for programmers.

**Multi-Timeframe Confluence Analysis**: TrendSpider's multi-timeframe confluence analysis is noted as best-in-class, automatically surfacing alignments across timeframes. MultiCharts can do this but requires manual chart setup or scripting. Winner: TrendSpider.

## Who Should Choose MultiCharts

- **Position traders backtesting portfolio strategies**: The genetic algorithm optimization for portfolio-level backtesting is built for traders managing multiple positions and correlations simultaneously.
- **Traders committed to one platform long-term**: If you plan to use the platform for 3+ years, the $1,497 one-time fee becomes cheaper than subscriptions. Ideal for serious, professional traders.
- **EasyLanguage programmers or coders**: If you're comfortable scripting trading logic, MultiCharts' power, flexibility, and broker connectivity across dozens of platforms unlock advanced workflows.
- **Windows-primary traders who need direct broker execution**: If you're actively trading and want to avoid switching between analysis and execution platforms, MultiCharts' multi-broker integration eliminates tool-switching friction.

## Who Should Choose TrendSpider

- **Technical analysts who want to automate chart marking**: If manual trendline and S/R detection consumes your analysis time, TrendSpider's automated detection pays for itself immediately in hours saved.
- **Traders building strategies without coding**: The visual bot builder lets you create trading bots through UI, not code. Ideal for traders who want automation but lack programming skills.
- **Traders who want AI-powered analysis assistance**: Sidekick AI with live market data is genuinely useful for research and validation, not just a chatbot.
- **Traders on tight budgets or testing new strategies**: The $19 trial and $54/month subscription remove the $1,497 barrier to entry, perfect for swing traders or retail traders bootstrapping accounts.

## The Verdict

MultiCharts wins for serious, portfolio-focused position traders planning a long-term commitment and who need direct broker integration. TrendSpider wins for technical analysts who want AI-powered charting automation and modern tools without paying a massive upfront fee. If you're actively trading multiple positions and have the capital to invest upfront, MultiCharts' portfolio-level backtesting and broker connectivity justify the $1,497 license. If you're a chart-focused trader who values time savings and visual automation, TrendSpider's $54/month subscription and best-in-class trendline detection are worth the ongoing cost.

title: My Funded Futures vs OneFunded (2026) — Which Is Better?
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and OneFunded — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures and OneFunded serve the same core audience—retail traders seeking prop firm funding—but take fundamentally different approaches. My Funded Futures specializes exclusively in futures trading with aggressive evaluation terms, faster payouts, and higher monthly fees, while OneFunded offers a lower-cost, multi-strategy entry point centered on forex and EAs with extended evaluation windows. This comparison examines which firm aligns with different trader profiles and trading objectives.

## Pricing Comparison

OneFunded undercuts My Funded Futures significantly on entry cost: $23/month versus $77/month—a 70% price difference. For a trader evaluating multiple challenges, this compounds quickly. OneFunded's challenge fee is refundable after the first payout, meaning successful traders recover their initial investment. My Funded Futures charges a flat $77/month with no mention of refunds on the evaluation tier, making failed evaluations a sunk cost.

However, My Funded Futures explicitly eliminates activation fees entirely—a $149–$300+ charge common in the prop firm industry. OneFunded doesn't mention activation fees, but their pricing structure suggests they're not waived. For traders planning to pass evaluation and trade live, My Funded Futures' monthly fee becomes irrelevant once funded, while OneFunded's structure remains unclear on whether subscription costs continue post-evaluation.

OneFunded's pricing advantage narrows when accounting for additional costs. Weekly payouts (beyond the standard frequency) require a paid upgrade—the price of which isn't disclosed in the materials provided. My Funded Futures advertises transparent payout speeds (15–20 minutes for the first payout) included in the base subscription.

**Winner on cost:** OneFunded for initial evaluation ($23 vs $77), but My Funded Futures for long-term funded trading due to eliminated activation fees and faster, guaranteed payout speeds.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Evaluation Structure and Pass Rate**  
My Funded Futures delivers a ~25% pass rate—roughly double the industry average—with a simplified one-phase evaluation. OneFunded's pass rate isn't disclosed, making it harder to assess evaluation difficulty objectively. One-phase evaluations typically mean fewer rules and faster qualification paths. OneFunded's "no time limits" advantage is overstated for most traders; evaluation windows measured in weeks or months don't materially change outcomes for disciplined traders, but they do extend capital commitment.

**Payout Speed**  
My Funded Futures guarantees payouts in 15–20 minutes. OneFunded's default appears to be weekly payouts, with faster frequencies requiring paid upgrades. For active traders running multiple evaluation cycles, My Funded Futures' speed is operationally significant—capital can redeploy faster.

**Market Coverage**  
My Funded Futures is futures-only: no stocks, forex, options, or crypto. This is the firm's most severe limitation. OneFunded supports forex and EAs via MetaTrader 4, though MetaTrader 5 is listed as "coming soon." Neither platform offers stocks or options, but OneFunded's forex coverage captures a larger swath of retail traders. However, OneFunded's scope is narrower than suggested—they explicitly exclude stocks, futures, and options.

**Trading Flexibility and Rules**  
My Funded Futures enforces no consistency rules on funded Pro accounts, allowing traders to take risks freely once funded. OneFunded permits EAs, copy trading, and news trading—a strong advantage for systematic traders. However, My Funded Futures' lack of consistency rules is more valuable post-funding, while OneFunded's strategy flexibility applies during evaluation. Both offer up to 90% profit splits, identical on this metric.

**Technology and Mobile Trading**  
Both claim mobile app support, but My Funded Futures' mobile trading depends entirely on Tradovate (a third-party platform), not a proprietary app. OneFunded operates via MetaTrader 4, the industry standard for mobile forex trading. This gives OneFunded a technical edge for on-the-go traders; Tradovate's mobile experience may lag MT4's familiarity for forex traders.

**Company Maturity**  
My Funded Futures (founded 2023) is newer but boasts a 4.4/5 rating. OneFunded's founding date isn't specified, but both are described as "relatively new" with "limited track records." My Funded Futures' higher rating suggests faster traction or better user satisfaction despite being younger.

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **Futures traders with capital urgency:** If you trade index futures (ES, NQ) or commodity futures (crude, gold) and need rapid capital deployment, the 15–20 minute payout speed and simplified one-phase evaluation justify the $77/month cost.

- **Traders comfortable with market specialization:** You're not diversifying across forex, stocks, and options; you're committed to futures. The lack of multi-market confusion means simpler risk management and clearer evaluation metrics.

- **Experience levels with a 50%+ win rate in backtests:** The 25% pass rate suggests My Funded Futures attracts more experienced traders or the evaluation is genuinely easier than competitors. If you're evaluating at other firms and passing, My Funded Futures' lower cost-to-fund may offset the monthly fee.

- **Traders planning long-term funded accounts:** Once funded, the elimination of activation fees and monthly subscription costs (implied by the funded path after 5 consecutive payouts) makes this firm's long-term cost structure competitive.

## Who Should Choose OneFunded

- **Forex traders and EA developers:** MetaTrader 4 and full EA support position OneFunded for systematic traders building algorithmic strategies. The $23 entry cost lowers the risk of testing an EA portfolio.

- **Budget-conscious evaluators testing multiple firms:** At $23/month, you can evaluate OneFunded alongside competitors without overcommitting capital. The challenge fee refund after the first payout reduces downside risk.

- **Copy trading enthusiasts:** If you rely on community strategies or copy trading, OneFunded's explicit permission for this approach (versus My Funded Futures' silence) is a key differentiator.

- **Traders wanting evaluation flexibility:** No time limits appeal to part-time traders or those building strategies across multiple sessions. However, this advantage is marginal unless you're chronically time-pressed.

## The Verdict

Choose **My Funded Futures** if you trade futures exclusively and prioritize speed (fast payouts, high pass rate, streamlined evaluation). The $77/month cost is offset by eliminated activation fees, faster capital access, and a 4.4/5 rating indicating strong execution. Choose **OneFunded** if you trade forex, use EAs or copy trading, and want minimal financial commitment while evaluating a prop firm—the $23/month entry and refundable fees make it a low-risk exploratory platform. For serious futures traders, My Funded Futures' speed and transparency outweigh OneFunded's lower cost.

title: My Funded Futures vs Phidias PropFirm (2026) — Which Is Better?
target_keyword: my funded futures vs phidias propfirm
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and Phidias PropFirm — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures and Phidias PropFirm are both futures-focused proprietary trading firms launched in 2023 offering quick access to funded accounts with high profit splits. Both emphasize speed and transparency, but they diverge significantly on pricing structure, evaluation difficulty, and drawdown rules. My Funded Futures targets traders seeking the lowest barrier to entry and highest probability of passing evaluation, while Phidias appeals to disciplined traders who want to pay once and trade indefinitely without monthly subscription pressure.

## Pricing Comparison

My Funded Futures charges $77/month with non-refundable fees if you fail the evaluation. This is a pure monthly subscription model—you pay regardless of whether you pass or trade, making failed attempts costly. There are no additional activation or data feed costs.

Phidias PropFirm costs $55/month but operates on a fundamentally different model: you pay a one-time evaluation fee and then trade without ongoing monthly charges on funded accounts. The $55 figure appears to be the monthly subscription cost, but Phidias explicitly advertises "no monthly subscription, activation, or data feed costs" on live accounts. This creates a critical distinction: if you pass evaluation on your first attempt, Phidias becomes $0/month ongoing, while My Funded Futures costs $77/month indefinitely.

**Value winner: Phidias for traders who pass quickly. My Funded Futures for traders practicing multiple evaluation attempts.** If you need 2-3 attempts to pass, My Funded Futures costs $154-231 total ($77 × failed attempts + $77 for passing account). Phidias costs $55 regardless of attempts. However, My Funded Futures' 25% pass rate (nearly double the 12-13% industry average) means fewer failed attempts in practice.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Evaluation Model & Pass Rates**
My Funded Futures uses a simplified one-phase evaluation with a 25% pass rate—significantly better odds than legacy competitors. Phidias hasn't published pass rate data. For risk-averse traders, My Funded Futures' higher pass probability translates to reaching funded status faster, offsetting its higher subscription cost.

**Drawdown Rules & Consistency Requirements**
My Funded Futures explicitly states "no consistency rules on funded Pro accounts," giving traders complete freedom on daily/weekly loss limits once funded. Phidias uses EOD (end-of-day) drawdown calculation, meaning intraday losses that recover by close don't count against your drawdown limit. This is more lenient than daily-reset rules but stricter than no rules. Phidias' approach favors scalpers and day traders who can recover intraday; My Funded Futures' lack of rules favors swing traders and position traders.

**Payout Speed & Structure**
My Funded Futures advertises payouts "in as little as 15–20 minutes" with a clear 5-consecutive-payout path to live trading accounts. Phidias offers "1–4 hour payouts via proprietary Phidias Wallet" on live accounts with no payout caps, plus daily withdrawals instead of batch weekly payouts. My Funded Futures' sub-20-minute claim is faster on paper, but Phidias' daily withdrawal flexibility for live accounts is more practical for full-time traders managing cash flow.

**Profit Splits**
Both offer up to 90% profit splits, functionally identical. Phidias explicitly promises "no payout caps" on live accounts; My Funded Futures doesn't mention caps but does mention different account tiers (Pro vs. standard), suggesting potential restrictions on lower tiers. Neither firm discloses the exact performance threshold required to achieve maximum splits.

**Platform & Mobile Access**
Both firms offer AI analysis, backtesting, paper trading, alerts, and API access. My Funded Futures relies on Tradovate's mobile app (a well-established third-party platform), while Phidias requires compatibility with Rithmic platforms. Neither has a proprietary mobile app. My Funded Futures' Tradovate integration is more accessible for beginners; Phidias' Rithmic requirement adds setup friction.

**Regulatory & Longevity**
Both firms are young (founded 2023) with limited long-term track records compared to 10+ year legacy competitors. My Funded Futures has a higher rating (4.4/5 vs. 3.9/5), suggesting better user satisfaction on core features like pass rates and payouts. Neither firm has faced regulatory action, but both carry inherent risk as new entities.

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **Traders practicing multiple evaluation attempts.** The 25% pass rate cuts evaluation costs significantly. Even at $77/month, failing twice on My Funded Futures ($154) still beats most competitors' single-attempt fees ($250-400).

- **New/intermediate traders risk-averse about drawdown limits.** No consistency rules on funded accounts mean you can trade your own edge without artificial daily/weekly loss restrictions once you're in.

## Who Should Choose Phidias PropFirm

## The Verdict

Choose **Phidias PropFirm** if you're confident in passing evaluation and want to minimize ongoing costs to nearly $0/month. title: My Funded Futures vs RebelsFunding (2026) — Which Is Better?
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and RebelsFunding — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures and RebelsFunding represent two distinct approaches to prop firm funding, each appealing to different trader profiles. My Funded Futures (4.4/5 rating) is a futures-focused firm prioritizing speed and simplicity—think fast payouts and no activation fees. RebelsFunding (3.9/5 rating) takes a challenge-based model with no time limits and ultra-low entry costs starting at $25. Both were founded in 2023 and share similar feature sets, but the core trade-off is specialization (futures depth) versus accessibility (low barriers to entry and unrestricted timelines).

## Pricing Comparison

My Funded Futures uses a subscription model: $77/month. This is non-refundable if your evaluation fails, meaning a trader loses $77 plus time if they don't pass. The monthly fee stacks; a trader taking 3 months to pass will spend $231 before earning funded capital.

RebelsFunding offers a free platform with pay-per-challenge entry fees starting at $25 for basic challenges. A trader pays only when attempting a challenge. The critical advantage: RebelsFunding refunds up to 200% of your challenge fee if you pass. Pass a $25 challenge and get $50 back—instant $25 profit before you trade. Pass a higher-tier challenge ($99 range) and receive $198-$297 in refunds. RebelsFunding has no monthly subscription drain.

**Value winner: RebelsFunding** if you eventually pass. A trader paying $25 per attempt and succeeding gets money back; My Funded Futures traders pay ongoing subscription fees regardless of outcome. However, if a My Funded Futures trader passes quickly (within one month), they spend just $77 vs. RebelsFunding's $25-$99 entry—negligible difference, but My Funded Futures' speed advantage (payout in 15-20 minutes) justifies the upfront cost for impatient traders.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Payout Speed:** My Funded Futures wins decisively—15-20 minute first payouts versus RebelsFunding's unspecified timeline. For traders managing cash flow or reinvesting profits, My Funded Futures' speed is a material advantage.

**Instrument Selection:** Futures only (My Funded Futures) versus multi-instrument (RebelsFunding). My Funded Futures eliminates asset class switching for traders committed to futures. RebelsFunding's broader selection matters for diversified traders, but "limited instrument selection compared to major competitors" suggests gaps that traders with specific strategies (e.g., forex, options) will notice.

**Profit Splits:** My Funded Futures offers up to 90% immediately. RebelsFunding scales from 80% to 90% after the first month. My Funded Futures' static 90% is slightly more transparent, but RebelsFunding's progression incentivizes consistency beyond one trade.

**Consistency Rules:** Both claim no consistency rules, maximizing flexibility for scalping, news trading, or erratic schedules.

**Evaluation Pass Rate:** My Funded Futures publishes ~25% pass rate (roughly double industry average). RebelsFunding doesn't publish pass rates publicly. My Funded Futures' transparency and higher pass rate suggest either easier standards or a larger trader base with fewer outliers. RebelsFunding's silence on this metric is a red flag for traders evaluating realistic odds.

**Account Progression:** My Funded Futures guarantees a live trading account after 5 consecutive payouts. RebelsFunding doesn't explicitly guarantee live trading progression. My Funded Futures' clear roadmap is psychologically and practically reassuring.

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **Futures traders committed to a single asset class.** If your entire strategy revolves around ES, NQ, MES, or crude oil, My Funded Futures eliminates irrelevant feature bloat. The platform and Tradovate integration are built for this niche.

- **Traders prioritizing speed and liquidity over low entry cost.** A $77/month subscription buys you access to faster payouts, higher listed pass rates, and a clearer progression path. For a trader planning to take 2-3 evaluation attempts, $154-$231 is a rounding error compared to months of lost opportunity cost.

- **Experienced traders with consistent profitability.** My Funded Futures' one-phase evaluation and 25% pass rate suit disciplined traders with proven track records. Beginners or struggling traders will waste the $77/month fee.

- **Traders who value transparency and firm stability.** A newer firm with published metrics (pass rate, payout speed, profit splits) and no hidden rules is lower-risk than a firm with opaque processes. My Funded Futures' directness appeals to risk-averse traders.

## Who Should Choose RebelsFunding

- **Budget-conscious traders testing the funding model.** A $25 entry fee with potential 200% refund on pass is zero-risk if you pass. Traders unsure whether prop funding fits their psychology or lifestyle can experiment cheaply.

- **Traders needing unlimited evaluation time.** Prop firms often impose 60-90 day challenge windows. RebelsFunding's no-time-limit structure suits traders with unpredictable schedules, caregiving responsibilities, or those who trade only part-time. Take as long as you need.

- **Multi-asset traders dabbling across stocks, forex, crypto, and indices.** If your strategy spans asset classes, RebelsFunding's broader instrument selection prevents the "forced specialization" problem My Funded Futures creates.

- **Traders seeking non-traditional platforms.** If you're comfortable outside MT4/MT5 ecosystems and don't require legacy integration, RebelsFunding's proprietary platform may feel fresher or offer unique features (though the limited instrument selection suggests limitations elsewhere too).

## The Verdict

**My Funded Futures wins for serious, focused futures traders** who want the fastest payouts and highest published pass rates, and can justify the $77/month subscription as the cost of operating a professional trading business. The 15-20 minute payout and clear live account progression path reward disciplined traders immediately.

**RebelsFunding wins for budget traders, part-time traders, or those exploring multiple asset classes**, where the $25 entry fee and up-to-200% refund eliminate financial risk, and unlimited challenge time beats arbitrary deadlines. However, the lack of published pass rates and MT4/5 support signals this firm is still finding its footing—use it as a low-cost testing ground, not a long-term home.

Choose My Funded Futures if you trade futures exclusively and value speed. Choose RebelsFunding if you're cost-conscious, instrument-flexible, or on your own timeline. Both are young firms (2023), so neither has legacy credibility; let the 4.4 vs. 3.9 rating and My Funded Futures' transparent metrics tip the scale for serious traders.

title: My Funded Futures vs SabioTrade (2026) — Which Is Better?
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and SabioTrade — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures and SabioTrade target the same trader demographic—those seeking prop firm funding without lengthy evaluation gauntlets—but diverge sharply on asset class support and payout mechanics. My Funded Futures specializes exclusively in futures trading with rapid payout cycles, while SabioTrade broadens its appeal to equity traders through a simplified one-step evaluation. For traders deciding between these two in 2026, the choice hinges on whether you trade futures or stocks, how urgently you need capital, and whether you value educational support alongside account access.

## Pricing Comparison

My Funded Futures charges $77 per month—the lower entry point by a significant margin. SabioTrade costs $119 per month, a 54% premium. On a 12-month commitment, that's $924 versus $1,428—a $504 difference that compounds if you pursue multiple evaluations.

However, SabioTrade's assessment fee is fully refundable after your first profitable payout, creating a conditional money-back mechanism if you generate profits. My Funded Futures offers no such refund guarantee; fees are non-refundable on failed evaluations. For traders with a 75% failure rate (typical in prop firms), this matters: a failed trader pays $77 with no recovery path at My Funded Futures, while SabioTrade traders who eventually succeed recoup their $119.

The real pricing distinction emerges in activation fees. My Funded Futures explicitly eliminates the $149–$300+ activation fees that competitors charge. SabioTrade's pricing structure doesn't mention activation fees, suggesting they may either bundle them into the monthly cost or waive them—but the product brief lacks clarity here. If SabioTrade charges hidden activation fees, the true cost advantage swings further toward My Funded Futures.

For a trader running three evaluation attempts over six months, My Funded Futures costs $462; SabioTrade costs $357 if one attempt succeeds and triggers the refund, or $714 if all fail. My Funded Futures remains cheaper in failure scenarios, but SabioTrade edges ahead if you achieve profitability on your first or second attempt.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Evaluation Structure & Pass Rates:** My Funded Futures operates a one-phase evaluation with a ~25% pass rate—approximately double the industry average. SabioTrade advertises a "1-step" evaluation but provides no published pass rate. Assuming industry-standard 12–15% pass rates, My Funded Futures gives traders twice the odds of approval. For risk-averse traders, a higher pass rate signals either more achievable metrics or a more forgiving evaluation—a meaningful psychological and financial advantage.

**Payout Speed:** My Funded Futures delivers first payouts in 15–20 minutes. SabioTrade processes weekly payouts within 24 hours. For a trader needing immediate access to profits—say, to re-deploy capital into a second account—My Funded Futures' near-instantaneous model is categorically superior. For longer-term funded traders, the payout frequency matters less; weekly cycles are standard in institutional trading. My Funded Futures wins on speed; SabioTrade wins on predictability (traders know exactly when to expect capital).

**Asset Class Coverage:** My Funded Futures exclusively offers futures trading. SabioTrade supports equities (stocks are implied by the product brief's silence on futures restrictions). For equity day traders or swing traders, SabioTrade is the only viable choice. For futures traders, My Funded Futures is the only option. This isn't a head-to-head feature—it's a dealbreaker for the wrong trader profile.

**Account Flexibility & Scaling:** My Funded Futures offers no consistency rules on funded Pro accounts, meaning traders enjoy full trading autonomy post-approval. SabioTrade funds accounts up to $650K but provides no scaling plans to grow funded account size. My Funded Futures outlines a clear path to a "real live trading account" after five consecutive payouts—a bridge to full proprietary trading. SabioTrade's lack of scaling suggests traders are capped at their initial funded account size.

**Education & Support:** SabioTrade includes free education and mentorship with all accounts. My Funded Futures emphasizes payout speed and evaluation pass rates but doesn't highlight educational components. For novice traders, SabioTrade's included mentorship adds tangible value beyond capital access; for experienced traders, it's superfluous.

**Profit Split:** Both offer 90% profit splits on qualifying accounts. My Funded Futures grants 90% splits across its tiers; SabioTrade specifies 90% splits on accounts $100K and above. For traders seeking sub-$100K funding, SabioTrade's split terms aren't stated—implying it may be lower. My Funded Futures is transparent on this front.

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **Futures-focused day and swing traders** seeking rapid capital deployment and weekly profit withdrawals. The 15–20 minute payout cycle is unmatched and ideal for traders who want to compound gains across multiple accounts.
  
- **Traders with a 12–18 month timeframe to profitability.** The higher 25% pass rate reduces evaluation friction, and the clear escalation path to live trading rewards consistency.

- **Cost-conscious traders running multiple evaluation attempts.** At $77/month with no activation fees, back-to-back evaluations stay affordable even through a 1–2 failure cycle.

- **Self-directed traders without need for formal education.** If you have a proven edge and simply need capital to scale it, My Funded Futures' stripped-down model—evaluation, payout, live account—executes that mission efficiently.

## Who Should Choose SabioTrade

- **Equity traders** seeking prop funding. My Funded Futures doesn't support stocks, making SabioTrade the only option for this critical trader segment.

- **Traders prioritizing educational support and mentorship.** If you're building a trading methodology, SabioTrade's included education accelerates the learning curve.

- **Traders seeking refundable evaluation fees.** The $119 assessment fee is fully refundable after your first profitable payout, reducing downside risk if you eventually succeed.

- **Traders comfortable with weekly payout cycles.** If you don't need immediate access to profits and prefer predictable (weekly) capital delivery, SabioTrade's rhythm is reliable.

## The Verdict

My Funded Futures wins decisively for futures traders who prioritize speed, higher approval odds, and transparent scaling. Its 25% pass rate and 15–20 minute payouts are industry-leading; the $77 monthly cost compounds that advantage. SabioTrade wins for equity traders—it's the only option here—and for traders who value education and the psychological benefit of refundable fees. The rating gap (4.4 vs 3.8) reflects My Funded Futures' stronger operational execution, but SabioTrade's payout disputes on Trustpilot warrant caution if you prioritize reliability over speed. Choose My Funded Futures if you trade futures and want the fastest capital-to-profits pipeline in 2026. Choose SabioTrade only if you trade stocks or need educational handholding; the $42/month premium is expensive for what you're gaining.

title: My Funded Futures vs SMB Capital (2026) — Which Is Better?
target_keyword: my funded futures vs smb capital
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and SMB Capital — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures and SMB Capital represent two fundamentally different paths to profitable trading. My Funded Futures is a futures prop firm charging $77/month with a single-phase evaluation structure and rapid funding—designed for traders who want immediate capital access with minimal barriers to entry. SMB Capital is a training and mentorship platform from an established proprietary trading firm, offering education-first access to trading capital through Mike Bellafiore's proven methodology, at no upfront cost but with substantial program fees ranging $5,000–$10,000+. The choice between them hinges on whether you prioritize speed-to-funding and simplicity (My Funded Futures) or structured professional development with mentorship (SMB Capital).

## Pricing Comparison

My Funded Futures operates on a straightforward monthly subscription model: $77/month for access to the evaluation. There is no activation fee, which eliminates the $149–$300+ charge imposed by most prop firms—a significant advantage for capital-conscious traders. Importantly, these monthly fees are non-refundable if you fail the evaluation, so a failed attempt costs $77 with no recovery.

SMB Capital's pricing structure is deceptive at first glance: the core platform is free. However, their professional training programs cost $5,000–$10,000+ and are required to access meaningful mentorship and the pathway to funded capital. This creates a hidden cost barrier—a trader entering SMB Capital should budget $5,000–$10,000 upfront and commit to the training curriculum before they ever see a trading account. No free trial or money-back guarantee is mentioned on their website, and pricing transparency is notably absent from their public materials.

**Winner on cost:** My Funded Futures for traders under $5,000 budget. A trader can attempt the evaluation for $77 and know exactly what they'll spend. SMB Capital only wins financially if you're willing to invest $5,000–$10,000 in education first.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Evaluation Speed & Path to Capital**

My Funded Futures delivers funded accounts in 15–20 minutes after passing evaluation. SMB Capital's timeline is undefined—it depends on completing paid training programs, demonstrating profitability in their trading room, and then waiting for allocation. My Funded Futures' advantage is measurable: same-day capital vs. months of training.

**Pass Rate & Accessibility**

My Funded Futures reports a ~25% pass rate, roughly double the industry average. This matters: statistically, you're twice as likely to pass here as at competitors. SMB Capital doesn't publish pass rates, making it impossible to gauge your realistic odds. However, their selective nature (NYC-centric, expensive programs, professional focus) implies a lower acceptance rate by design.

**Trading Instrument Coverage**

My Funded Futures is futures-only—ES (S&P 500 futures), NQ (Nasdaq futures), and micro contracts. If you trade stocks, forex, options, or crypto, you cannot use this platform. SMB Capital's feature list mirrors My Funded Futures' capabilities, but doesn't explicitly specify instrument restrictions, suggesting broader coverage aligned with traditional prop trading (stocks, options, futures).

**Profit Splits & Consistency Rules**

My Funded Futures offers up to 90% profit splits with zero consistency rules on funded accounts—you can have a losing day on a funded account without immediate breach. SMB Capital's splits and rules are not specified in available materials, a red flag for transparency.

**Mobile Trading & Proprietary Tools**

My Funded Futures depends on Tradovate's mobile app—no proprietary offering—which means you're using a third-party broker interface. SMB Capital's mobile capabilities are unspecified. Neither platform appears to deliver in-house mobile trading software, a minor disadvantage for active traders who prioritize mobility.

**Mentorship & Training Quality**

SMB Capital's advantage: Mike Bellafiore is a recognized trader and the PlayBook methodology has proven track record across multiple market cycles. My Funded Futures offers no formal training—you're evaluated on raw trading ability, with no mentorship structure.

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **Traders with proven track records** who need capital fast and don't require hand-holding. You've paper-traded or backtested successfully; you just need money to put to work.

- **Futures-focused day and swing traders** specifically targeting ES, NQ, or micro contracts. If your edge works exclusively on index futures, this is the straightest path to funding.

- **Capital-constrained traders** with $77–$500 total budget. You cannot afford $5,000+ programs but can afford to attempt multiple evaluations ($77 × 3 or 4 attempts).

- **Traders who value transparency and speed over mentorship**. You want to know exactly what you're paying, see funded accounts in minutes, and trade without consistency constraints.

## Who Should Choose SMB Capital

- **Developing traders willing to invest in structured education** who recognize that $5,000–$10,000 in training is cheaper than 12 months of learning-by-losing in a real account.

- **Traders seeking mentorship and a trading community** within an established prop firm ecosystem. You want accountability, trading room access, and connection to professional traders.

- **Traders targeting stocks and options** where SMB Capital likely has deeper institutional knowledge and capital allocation than My Funded Futures' futures-only offering.

- **Traders in or willing to relocate to NYC** for in-person programs. Remote access is available but the premium experience and network require physical presence.

## The Verdict

My Funded Futures wins for traders seeking speed, simplicity, and low upfront cost—pay $77/month, pass a one-phase evaluation, receive capital in 15–20 minutes, and trade with 90% splits and no consistency rules. SMB Capital wins for traders who view funded trading as the endpoint of structured professional development and are willing to invest $5,000–$10,000 upfront for elite training, mentorship, and a proven trading methodology. Choose My Funded Futures if you're capital-ready and need speed; choose SMB Capital if you're still building your edge and need guidance from seasoned professionals.

title: My Funded Futures vs TakeProfitTrader (2026) — Which Is Better?
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and TakeProfitTrader — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures and TakeProfitTrader are both futures-exclusive prop trading platforms designed for swing traders and day traders seeking capital-free evaluation paths and profit-sharing arrangements. Both charge monthly subscriptions for simulated trading accounts with evaluation milestones that unlock funded live trading. The primary differences lie in payout structure, evaluation rules, and startup costs—making this a choice between faster initial access (My Funded Futures) and daily liquidity with higher customer satisfaction scores (TakeProfitTrader).

## Pricing Comparison

My Funded Futures costs $77/month with zero activation or setup fees—a structural advantage that saves traders $149–$300 upfront compared to competitors. TakeProfitTrader charges $90/month, adding $13/month or $156/year compared to My Funded Futures. Neither platform offers free trials or money-back guarantees; fees are non-refundable regardless of evaluation pass/fail outcome.

For traders planning a 6-month evaluation window, the cumulative cost difference is $78 in My Funded Futures' favor. However, TakeProfitTrader's $50 withdrawal fee on payouts under $250 creates hidden costs for smaller daily winners—a monthly fee that could exceed the monthly subscription disadvantage for traders withdrawing multiple small payouts weekly.

If a trader takes 15 days to pass evaluation and then withdraws twice weekly with average payouts of $180, TakeProfitTrader adds $400/month in withdrawal fees alone. In this scenario, My Funded Futures' lower subscription cost becomes secondary to TakeProfitTrader's withdrawal penalty structure. For traders withdrawing payouts above $250 consistently, pricing converges.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Payout Speed & Withdrawal Limits:** My Funded Futures advertises first payouts in 15–20 minutes with unspecified withdrawal caps on funded accounts. TakeProfitTrader processes daily payouts with explicitly zero maximum withdrawal limits—traders can withdraw all daily profits without caps. For swing traders holding positions 3–5 days, My Funded Futures' speed matters less. For day traders closing positions daily, TakeProfitTrader's daily payout structure with unlimited caps is structurally superior, eliminating the need to batch withdrawals.

**Evaluation Rules & Restrictions:** My Funded Futures uses a simplified one-phase eval with no consistency rules on funded Pro accounts—traders can switch between scalping, swing trading, or position trading without penalty. TakeProfitTrader imposes a 50% consistency rule requiring traders to maintain at least 50% of their highest daily profit in subsequent sessions. For aggressive traders or those testing multiple strategies during evaluation, My Funded Futures removes friction; TakeProfitTrader's rule forces traders into narrower tactical boxes, particularly if an initial winning session was anomalously large.

**Evaluation Pass Rate & Time:** My Funded Futures reports a ~25% pass rate—roughly double the industry average—suggesting either lenient rules or a real trader-friendly design. TakeProfitTrader offers a simple one-step eval with a 6% profit target and no time limit, but doesn't publish pass rates. The unlimited timeline is a pro for underfunded traders; it's a con for those wanting certainty of evaluation length. My Funded Futures' higher published pass rate suggests faster average evaluation completion.

**Reputation & Social Proof:** TakeProfitTrader holds a 4.4/5 Trustpilot rating from 8,300+ reviews with 81% five-star ratings—substantial third-party validation. My Funded Futures rates 4.4/5 but with unspecified review volume, suggesting a smaller user base or newer firm effect. TakeProfitTrader's review volume provides stronger confidence in consistency of the trading experience.

**Mobile & API Access:** Both platforms claim mobile_app, alerts, api_access, and broker_integration. Both depend on Tradovate for mobile trading rather than proprietary apps. No functional differentiation here.

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **New to prop trading without capital:** The $77/month with zero activation fees makes entry accessible. Traders paying their own evaluation cost (not funded by brokers) save $200+ immediately.

- **Flexible strategy traders:** If you scalp one week, swing trade the next, then hold overnight, the absence of consistency rules lets you trade naturally without penalty. TakeProfitTrader's 50% consistency rule is a hardline constraint that this profile violates.

- **Traders seeking fastest evaluation completion:** The 25% pass rate (double industry average) suggests shorter eval cycles on average. For impatient traders or those with limited trial periods, My Funded Futures likely closes evaluations faster.

- **Traders wanting clear path to live accounts:** The explicit "5 consecutive payouts = real live account access" roadmap is transparent. You know the goal post and the milestone. TakeProfitTrader doesn't publish this clarity.

## Who Should Choose TakeProfitTrader

- **Day traders who withdraw daily:** The unlimited daily payout cap with no maximum withdrawal limits makes TakeProfitTrader structurally superior for traders closing all positions daily and withdrawing profits. My Funded Futures' unspecified withdrawal caps create uncertainty.

- **Traders who value external validation:** Trustpilot's 8,300+ reviews with 81% five-star provide third-party confidence. If you're skeptical of marketing claims, TakeProfitTrader's review volume reduces execution risk perception.

- **Traders indifferent to evaluation time:** The one-step eval with no time limit removes pressure. If you're learning, iterating, or building discipline slowly, the unlimited timeline is a feature, not a bug. My Funded Futures' faster evals assume you're ready to prove profitability quickly.

- **Traders with stable, consistent strategies:** If your strategy generates reliably similar daily profits (the opposite of aggressive variance), the 50% consistency rule isn't a burden—it's alignment. Disciplined traders won't hit this limit.

## The Verdict

My Funded Futures wins for capital-constrained traders and flexible strategy testers; the $77/month baseline with zero activation fees and no consistency rules cuts friction. TakeProfitTrader wins for daily-active traders and those seeking pattern validation; the unlimited daily payouts, zero withdrawal caps, and 4.4/5 Trustpilot rating from 8,300+ reviews outweigh the $13/month premium and 50% consistency constraint for disciplined day traders. Choose My Funded Futures if you're optimizing for lowest cost and fastest evaluation. Choose TakeProfitTrader if you trade daily and want proven, third-party-validated execution without withdrawal penalties on sub-$250 payouts.

- **Traders who value established MT4/MT5 ecosystems**. If you've built years of Expert Advisors, custom indicators, or templates in MetaTrader, The Funded Trader's native support eliminates platform migration friction.
- **Ambitious traders targeting six-figure funded accounts**. The explicit $600K scaling plan with proven payout history (despite delays) provides a clear roadmap for capital growth that My Funded Futures doesn't articulate.
- **Traders with higher risk tolerance and patience**. If payout delays and rule changes don't deter you, The Funded Trader's broader asset range and larger maximum account size justify staying despite its lower 80% profit split and slower customer support.

## The Verdict

**My Funded Futures wins for cost, speed, and simplicity**—pick it if you trade futures exclusively and want no-nonsense payouts, lower total entry cost, and higher odds of approval. The 15–20 minute payout speed and absence of activation fees make it the most trader-friendly entry point in the industry. **The Funded Trader wins for flexibility and ambition**—choose it if you trade multiple asset classes, use MetaTrader platforms, or aim to scale to $600K+. The price advantage is negligible once activation fees enter the equation, and The Funded Trader's platform depth justifies the trade-off in profit split if you're not futures-only. The decisive factor: Do you specialize in futures (My Funded Futures) or need multi-asset flexibility (The Funded Trader)?

title: My Funded Futures vs The5ers (2026) — Which Is Better?
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and The5ers — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures and The5ers are both established prop trading firms, but they serve distinctly different trader profiles. My Funded Futures specializes in futures trading with a focus on speed, accessibility, and high pass rates, while The5ers targets forex traders seeking maximum scaling potential and profit-share flexibility. Both firms have solid ratings (4.4/5 and 4.2/5 respectively), but their core mechanics—evaluation difficulty, payout structure, and asset class focus—make them suited to different trading styles and experience levels.

## Pricing Comparison

My Funded Futures charges a flat $77 per month subscription with zero activation fees. This is crucial: most prop firms charge $149–$300+ in upfront activation fees on top of monthly costs, making them functionally more expensive in year one. For a trader taking the evaluation, My Funded Futures' monthly-only model reduces total barrier to entry significantly.

The5ers costs $95 per month, also with no hidden activation fees mentioned. At $18 more per month than My Funded Futures, this is a 23% premium. Over a 12-month period, you'd pay $924 (MFF) versus $1,140 (The5ers)—a $216 difference that accumulates fast if you're cycling through multiple evaluation attempts.

Neither firm mentions free trials or money-back guarantees. Both charge non-refundable subscriptions if you fail the evaluation, meaning failed traders lose their monthly fee regardless of broker selection. This is standard but worth noting: you're not getting your $77 or $95 back if you blow the account. The pricing edge goes decisively to My Funded Futures—lower entry cost, same feature set, faster path to profitability.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Payout Speed and Accessibility**: My Funded Futures delivers first payouts in 15–20 minutes—a critical advantage for traders who want proof their prop firm actually delivers. The5ers doesn't specify payout timing, which is a red flag; most forex-focused firms take 3–7 days. Speed matters psychologically and operationally. My Funded Futures wins.

**Evaluation Pass Rate**: My Funded Futures reports a ~25% pass rate, roughly double the industry average of 12–15%. The5ers doesn't publish pass rates, which typically means they're below the industry baseline. If you're evaluating your trading skill, a 25% pass rate signals you have a reasonable shot, while a hidden pass rate suggests harder conditions. Significant advantage: My Funded Futures.

**Profit Splits**: The5ers offers up to 100% profit splits after meeting consistency rules and scaling targets. My Funded Futures offers up to 90% on funded Pro accounts. The 10% difference matters on large accounts—on a $500K account earning $10K/month, that's a $1K/month gap. However, The5ers requires scaling and consistency; My Funded Futures explicitly has no consistency rules on funded accounts. The5ers' higher split is undermined by the strings attached. Edge: My Funded Futures for flexibility.

**Asset Class Coverage**: This is the deciding factor for many. My Funded Futures is futures-only—no stocks, forex, options, or crypto. The5ers is forex-focused, though it doesn't explicitly exclude other assets. If you trade futures, My Funded Futures is purpose-built. If you trade forex, The5ers is the natural fit. If you want multi-asset flexibility, neither perfectly serves you, but The5ers is the broader option. Slight edge: The5ers for diversification.

**Trading Platform Dependence**: My Funded Futures has no proprietary mobile app and depends on Tradovate for mobile trading. The5ers doesn't specify, but established forex firms typically use cTrader or MT4/MT5. My Funded Futures' reliance on a third-party broker's app is a weakness for traders who need seamless mobile execution. Edge: The5ers (likely).

**Track Record and Stability**: My Funded Futures launched in 2023—three years old as of 2026. The5ers has operated since 2016, giving it 10 years of track record. For capital deployment, longevity matters. Firms that survive 5+ years without collapsing typically have solid risk management. The5ers' longer tenure reduces operational risk. Edge: The5ers.

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **Futures-specific traders** who want a firm optimized for their asset class. You're trading ES, NQ, CL, or other futures contracts, and you need a prop firm that doesn't force you into forex or stocks.
- **Speed-focused traders** who need fast payouts to reinvest capital. If you're using funded profits for compounding or living expenses, the 15–20 minute payout window is a game-changer compared to week-long delays elsewhere.
- **Newer traders evaluating themselves** who benefit from a higher pass rate. A 25% pass rate means you have a real shot at demonstrating your strategy works before committing larger personal capital to the firm.
- **Cost-conscious evaluators** taking multiple attempts. At $77/month with no activation fee, repeated evaluations cost less than firms charging $250+ per try. If you're in development phase, this matters.

## Who Should Choose The5ers

- **Forex traders** who want an established, forex-native prop firm. You're trading majors, minors, or exotics, and you want a firm with decade-long forex expertise and relationship capital with liquidity providers.
- **Traders targeting maximum scaling**. The5ers' ability to scale up to $4M is industry-leading. If your edge generates consistent, repeatable profits, you want access to that capital pool without waiting years.
- **Profit-split negotiators** seeking 100% splits. Once you prove consistency and stability, The5ers can deliver full profit retention—effectively making you a self-funded trader with their infrastructure. This is unique leverage.
- **Risk-averse traders** who prioritize firm stability. The5ers' 10-year operating history and established reputation reduce counterparty risk. You're betting on a firm that's survived market cycles and regulatory scrutiny.

## The Verdict

Choose **My Funded Futures** if you trade futures and want the fastest path from evaluation to payout with minimal cost friction. The $77 monthly fee, 25% pass rate, and 15–20 minute payouts make it the most accessible prop firm for futures traders as of 2026. Skip it if you trade forex or need multi-asset support.

Choose **The5ers** if you're a forex trader seeking maximum profit-share potential and don't mind a slightly higher $95 monthly cost for access to scaling beyond $1M and a firm with proven decade-long stability. The trade-off is longer payout timing and stricter consistency requirements, but the rewards scale accordingly.

The clear differentiator: **My Funded Futures wins on speed and cost for futures**; **The5ers wins on scale and stability for forex**. Your asset class choice determines the winner.

title: My Funded Futures vs ThinkCapital (2026) — Which Is Better?
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and ThinkCapital — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures is a streamlined futures-focused prop firm launched in 2023, designed for traders seeking rapid account funding with minimal upfront costs and straightforward evaluation mechanics. ThinkCapital, backed by the regulated broker ThinkMarkets, offers a multi-step challenge system across 4,000+ instruments including forex, indices, commodities, and crypto, appealing to diversified traders who want broker stability alongside challenge-based funding. The choice between them hinges on asset class preferences, evaluation flexibility, and long-term broker reliability.

## Pricing Comparison

My Funded Futures charges $77 per month with no activation fees—a critical cost advantage over competitors charging $149–$300 upfront. The monthly fee applies whether you pass or fail evaluation, making it non-refundable on unsuccessful attempts.

ThinkCapital's base challenge costs $39 per month, roughly half My Funded Futures' price. However, unlocking the 90% profit split (matching My Funded Futures) requires purchasing an add-on costing approximately 25% more than the base fee, bringing the effective total to around $49–$50 monthly. ThinkCapital's scaling path offers frequent 25–40% promotional discounts when stepping up from $25K to higher capital allocations.

**Value calculation:** A trader attempting My Funded Futures three times would spend $231; three ThinkCapital attempts with the 90% split add-on would cost $147–$150. ThinkCapital is cheaper at entry for equal profit splits, but My Funded Futures' no-activation-fee structure saves new traders significant money compared to legacy competitors. For budget-conscious prop traders, ThinkCapital wins on raw pricing; for those avoiding legacy firm activation fees entirely, My Funded Futures' transparent monthly model is preferable.

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Evaluation Structure & Pass Rate:** My Funded Futures offers a single simplified phase with approximately 25% evaluation pass rate—roughly double the industry average—making it statistically easier to fund. ThinkCapital provides 1-, 2-, and 3-step formats, offering flexibility but requiring traders to select their own difficulty tier. My Funded Futures' standardized approach removes decision paralysis; ThinkCapital's multi-format system lets experienced traders risk less capital ($25K) upfront but introduces choice friction. **Winner: My Funded Futures for straightforwardness; ThinkCapital for customization.**

**Asset Class Coverage:** My Funded Futures exclusively supports futures, eliminating the $149–$300+ activation fees from competing prop firms but locking traders into a single asset class. ThinkCapital supports 4,000+ instruments across forex, indices, commodities, crypto, and ETFs, all CFD-based through ThinkMarkets' regulated infrastructure. A forex scalper or crypto trader cannot use My Funded Futures; an options trader cannot use either (though ThinkCapital's indices may substitute). **Winner: ThinkCapital by necessity for diversified portfolios.**

**Broker Backing & Regulatory Standing:** ThinkCapital operates through ThinkMarkets, licensed by the FCA, ASIC, and CySEC with 10+ operating years. My Funded Futures, founded in 2023, has minimal public regulatory history and operates independently. For risk-averse traders or those in regulated jurisdictions, ThinkMarkets' multi-regulatory license provides tangible reassurance; My Funded Futures' newness introduces unknown tail risk around fund security and payout reliability. **Winner: ThinkCapital for institutional safety.**

**Payout Speed:** My Funded Futures advertises 15–20 minute first payouts, a measurable operational advantage over ThinkCapital's undisclosed payout timeline. Speed matters for active traders managing capital across multiple accounts. **Winner: My Funded Futures.**

**Platform & Integration Options:** My Funded Futures relies on Tradovate for mobile trading with no proprietary app—adequate for futures traders but limiting for multi-asset traders. ThinkCapital supports MT5, TradingView integration, and its proprietary ThinkTrader platform, offering serious traders multiple familiar ecosystems. **Winner: ThinkCapital for platform flexibility.**

**Post-Funding Path:** My Funded Futures offers progression to a live trading account after 5 consecutive payouts with no consistency rules on funded accounts, creating a clear graduation pathway. ThinkCapital scales to $1.5M allocated capital through paid step-ups but lacks explicit live account migration. **Winner: My Funded Futures for long-term account growth.**

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **Futures traders optimizing for cost and speed:** If you trade ES, NQ, CL, or other index/commodity futures exclusively, My Funded Futures eliminates the $149–$300 activation fees endemic to competitors while offering double the industry evaluation pass rate. Recoup the $77 monthly cost within your first profitable month.

- **Traders prioritizing rapid funding feedback:** The 15–20 minute payout window and single evaluation phase compress time-to-capital compared to 2–3 step evaluations elsewhere. If you need funded capital deployed quickly and can wait 30 days maximum for evaluation results, this firm's operational efficiency justifies the monthly fee.

- **New prop traders wary of legacy firm costs:** If you've been scared away from prop trading by $300 activation fees and vague scaling terms, My Funded Futures' transparent monthly model removes that friction. Pass or fail, you know exactly what you've paid and no hidden costs await.

- **Traders with consistent, rule-flexible strategies:** The no-consistency-rules policy on funded Pro accounts rewards discretionary traders and systematic traders with non-standard risk profiles that would violate competitor drawdown limits. If your strategy involves occasional 4–5% daily swings, this flexibility is irreplaceable.

## Who Should Choose ThinkCapital

- **Multi-asset traders seeking unified infrastructure:** Forex scalpers, index/commodity traders, and crypto enthusiasts need ThinkCapital's 4,000-instrument catalog and CFD access across asset classes. My Funded Futures cannot serve this profile; you lose flexibility entirely.

- **Risk-averse traders prioritizing broker stability:** ThinkMarkets' FCA/ASIC/CySEC licensing and 10-year operating history provide institutional reassurance that an independent 2023-founded futures firm cannot match. If regulatory oversight and payout security outweigh speed concerns, ThinkCapital's backing is worth the trade-off.

- **Capital-constrained traders testing multiple strategies:** The 1-step challenge at $25K entry cost (base fee $39) lets you test a single strategy cheaply before committing to larger allocations. ThinkCapital's promotional discounts (25–40% off) on scaling mean stepping up to $50K, $100K, or higher becomes progressively cheaper per dollar of capital allocated.

- **Traders comfortable with MT5 and TradingView ecosystems:** If your workflows already center on MT5 charting or TradingView alerts, ThinkCapital's native integration eliminates switching friction. My Funded Futures' Tradovate-only dependency may require relearning platform mechanics.

## The Verdict

**Choose My Funded Futures if you trade futures exclusively, prioritize low fees and fast payouts, and want a simplified path to live account funding.** The double-industry-average pass rate and no-activation-fee structure create genuine cost savings for futures traders, and the 15–20 minute payout timeline is operationally superior. However, accept the new-firm risk and futures-only limitation.

**Choose ThinkCapital if you trade multiple asset classes, demand regulatory broker backing, or need platform flexibility across MT5, TradingView, and proprietary systems.** The $39 base price is cheaper, scaling discounts reward capital growth, and ThinkMarkets' established regulatory presence matters if you're deploying significant capital. Trade evaluation simplicity for asset-class breadth and institutional reassurance.

For most futures traders under time/capital pressure, My Funded Futures wins. For diversified traders valuing stability, ThinkCapital is the more defensible choice.

title: My Funded Futures vs Topstep (2026) — Which Is Better?
summary: Compare My Funded Futures and Topstep — features, pricing, pros and cons.
analysis:
## Overview

My Funded Futures and Topstep are both futures-focused prop firms designed to help traders access capital and keep a significant share of their profits. My Funded Futures targets newer traders seeking fast access and lower entry costs, while Topstep appeals to serious traders who want structured development, active coaching, and a longer-established track record. Both offer funded accounts, but they differ significantly in evaluation difficulty, pricing, and philosophy.

## Pricing Comparison

My Funded Futures charges $77/month with no activation fees. This is the lowest barrier to entry: a trader can evaluate within their first month for less than $80 total.

Topstep charges $165/month, which is 114% higher. More critically, Topstep's fees accumulate during the entire evaluation period. If your evaluation takes 3 months, you've paid $495 before earning a single dollar. My Funded Futures' one-phase evaluation means faster movement through the program, reducing total upfront cost.

Neither platform offers refunds if you fail the evaluation. My Funded Futures' advantage here is mathematical: failing costs $77. Failing Topstep costs whatever you paid during the evaluation month.

For ongoing funded accounts: My Funded Futures offers up to 90% profit splits with no monthly fees on Pro accounts. Topstep's most attractive feature is a 100% profit split on the first $5,000 earned, but this applies only initially; the structure beyond that is less clear. Traders trading under $150K accounts will see better net returns with My Funded Futures after fees.

**Winner: My Funded Futures for cost. Topstep is only cheaper if you evaluate extremely quickly or plan to scale to very large accounts.**

## Key Features Head-to-Head

**Evaluation Pass Rate & Difficulty**: My Funded Futures claims a ~25% pass rate, roughly double the industry average. Topstep's pass rate is not disclosed publicly, but evaluation rules described as "restrictive" (particularly consistency requirements) suggest a lower rate. For traders who are moderately skilled but not elite, My Funded Futures is substantially more approachable.

**Payout Speed**: My Funded Futures guarantees first payouts in 15–20 minutes. Topstep offers "consistent and reliable" payouts but doesn't specify speed. In active trading, speed matters—a 15-minute payout versus a 2-day payout is the difference between deploying capital immediately and sitting in cash. My Funded Futures wins decisively here.

**Flexibility on Funded Accounts**: My Funded Futures explicitly allows trading without consistency rules on Pro accounts. Topstep enforces evaluation rules including consistency requirements, which restrict trading strategy on funded accounts. If you want to scalp, day-trade volatility, or trade news events without drawdown limits, My Funded Futures is your only option.

**Risk Management & Coaching**: Topstep emphasizes "strong risk management coaching." My Funded Futures doesn't highlight coaching in the same way. Traders seeking structured mentorship and development should lean Topstep. Traders confident in their own risk management will find My Funded Futures' lighter approach preferable.

**Community & Trading Rooms**: Topstep operates "live trading rooms" and an active community. My Funded Futures lists "social features" but not live trading venues. For accountability partners and real-time discussion, Topstep wins. For independent traders, this doesn't matter.

**Automated Trading**: Topstep prohibits automated trading. My Funded Futures' policy isn't stated, but the lack of explicit prohibition suggests it may be allowed. If you trade algorithmically or plan to build bots, My Funded Futures is the safer choice.

## Who Should Choose My Funded Futures

- **Budget-conscious evaluators**: If your total capital for evaluation is under $500, the $77 entry fee makes sense. You can afford to fail multiple times and still spend less than one Topstep evaluation cycle.

- **Traders seeking fast-track profitability**: If you want your first payout within days (not weeks), and need capital accessible immediately for deployment, My Funded Futures' 15–20 minute payout speed is a game-changer.

- **Flexible trading style traders**: Day scalpers, news traders, and volatility-focused traders who can't operate under consistency rules should start here. Topstep's restrictions would cripple your strategy.

- **New or algorithmic traders**: Founded in 2023, My Funded Futures is designed for modern trading (backtesting, API access, mobile-first). If you're building bots or testing strategies programmatically, the tooling feels newer and more aligned.

## Who Should Choose Topstep

- **Traders prioritizing risk management education**: If you want active coaching and structured trader development, Topstep's emphasis on mentorship is worth the $165/month. This isn't just platform access; it's a development program.

- **Established traders with capital**: If you've traded live for 2+ years, you don't need basic coaching. Topstep's $150K max account and strong payout history make it suitable for serious, capital-efficient traders who've already learned risk management elsewhere.

- **Traders who value legacy and trust**: Topstep is "one of the most established" prop firms with a longer operational history than My Funded Futures (founded 2023). For traders who prioritize institutional reputation over innovation, this matters.

- **Community-focused traders**: If live trading rooms, active community engagement, and real-time accountability with other traders influence your decision, Topstep's community infrastructure is the differentiator.

## The Verdict

**Choose My Funded Futures if you're price-sensitive, want fast payouts, trade with flexibility, or are new to prop trading.** The $77 entry, 15-minute payouts, and ~25% pass rate make it objectively more accessible than Topstep. For the vast majority of retail futures traders, the lower cost and faster money movement outweigh legacy reputation.

**Choose Topstep if you're an established trader seeking active coaching, community engagement, and don't mind premium pricing for institutional credibility.** The trade-off is clear: pay 2.1x more monthly, accept stricter evaluation rules, and receive mentorship in return.

My Funded Futures is the better tool for most traders evaluating today. Topstep is the better choice if development and community matter more than speed and cost.

title: My Funded Futures vs Trade The Pool (2026) — Which Is Better?
